   173	    /// hex if empty.
   174	    pub l4e_last_hash_hex: String,
   175	    /// One-line classification of the replay error if replay failed.
   176	    pub replay_failure: Option<String>,
   177	    /// True iff `<runtime_repo>/initial_q_state.json` was found and loaded.
   178	    /// False when the verifier defaulted to `QState::genesis()`.
   179	    pub initial_q_state_loaded_from_disk: bool,
   180	}
   181	
   182	impl ReplayReport {
   183	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 4 — ship-gate aggregator over the 5
   184	    /// architect-mandated boolean indicators. The CLI uses this to drive its
   185	    /// exit code (0 when all pass, 1 otherwise).
   186	    ///
   187	    /// True iff every architect-mandated boolean indicator is `true`.
   188	    /// **TB-7 Atom 4**: also checks the new `agent_signatures_verified` (Gate 4)
   189	    /// and `proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable` (Gate 5) indicators.
   190	    pub fn all_indicators_pass(&self) -> bool {
   191	        self.ledger_root_verified
   192	            && self.system_signatures_verified
   193	            && self.state_reconstructed
   194	            && self.economic_state_reconstructed
   195	            && self.cas_payloads_retrievable
   196	            && self.agent_signatures_verified
   197	            && self.proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable
   198	    }
   199	}
   200	
   201	// ── Verifier entry-points ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   202	
   203	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 4 — verify_chaintape options.
   204	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   205	pub struct VerifyOptions {
   206	    /// Optional run-id filter; if provided, the verifier asserts the
   207	    /// pinned-pubkey manifest's `run_id` matches before replay. None =
   208	    /// no filter (smoke evidence may legitimately not echo a run-id).
   209	    pub expected_run_id: Option<String>,
   210	}
   211	
   212	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 4 — single library entry-point for replay
   213	/// + signature + CAS + L4.E verification. The CLI binary at
   214	/// `src/bin/verify_chaintape.rs` is a thin wrapper around this.
   215	///
   216	/// Steps (mirrors architect § 3.6 Atom 4):
   217	/// 1. Read `pinned_pubkeys.json` from `runtime_repo_path`. Decode hex
   218	///    pubkey(s) into a `PinnedSystemPubkeys` map keyed by `SystemEpoch`.
   219	/// 2. Resolve initial `QState` from `<runtime_repo>/initial_q_state.json` if
   220	///    present; else `QState::genesis()`.

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba src/runtime/verify.rs | sed -n '500,526p'" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
 succeeded in 0ms:
   500	
   501	    (
   502	        agent_signatures_verified,
   503	        proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable,
   504	    )
   505	}
   506	
   507	// ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   508	
   509	fn decode_pubkey_hex(hex: &str) -> Result<Vec<u8>, VerifyError> {
   510	    if hex.len() % 2 != 0 {
   511	        return Err(VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(format!(
   512	            "odd-length hex string ({})",
   513	            hex.len()
   514	        )));
   515	    }
   516	    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(hex.len() / 2);
   517	    for chunk in hex.as_bytes().chunks(2) {
   518	        let s = std::str::from_utf8(chunk)
   519	            .map_err(|e| VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(e.to_string()))?;
   520	        let byte = u8::from_str_radix(s, 16)
   521	            .map_err(|e| VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(e.to_string()))?;
   522	        out.push(byte);
   523	    }
   524	    Ok(out)
   525	}
   526

     1	# TuringOS v4 — Genesis payload (Phase B7).
     2	#
     3	# Frozen at Phase B B7 (2026-04-25). Two binding sections:
     4	#

     7	#                         baseline_regression_jsonl_sha256 are placeholders
     8	#                         until B7-extra (p_0 calibration) lands.
     9	#

    11	#                         (`turingosv4::boot::verify_trust_root`) recomputes
    12	#                         each hash and aborts with TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED on
    13	#                         mismatch.
    14	#

    57	#     tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs (per CLAUDE.md "每个 ✅ 行 ≥1

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:77:Re-evaluate Q1-Q9 against round-5 HEAD. Also evaluate the round-5-specific risks:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:79:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. Q1 was PASS in round-3; round-5 doesn't change mint logic. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:83:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Same as Q2 above. Tests at `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs::sg_13_6` should still cover both mismatch cases.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:85:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` `total_supply_micro` + `assert_total_ctf_conserved`. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:87:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: `assert_complete_set_balanced` MIN form. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:89:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: `MarketSeedTx` accept arm. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:91:**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:93:**Q8 (underflow)**: Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:95:**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Round-5 added type-use discovery. Re-evaluate the fence's bypass surface. Specifically: with type-use walking, can a contributor still introduce a TB-13 contribution that bypasses both the marker AND type-use layers? E.g., a file that uses TB-13 types only via aliases (`use crate::state::typed_tx::CompleteSetMintTx as Foo;` then `let x: Foo = ...`) — does the type-use scan catch the `use ... as` line? It should because `CompleteSetMintTx` appears on the use-line itself, even though the bound name is `Foo`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:99:**Round-5 RQ3 smoke determinism**: The smoke pre-computes the post-mint state_root via `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx)`. If the function's hashing is somehow non-deterministic (timestamps, hashmap ordering), the redeem's parent_state_root would be wrong and the redeem would reject with StaleParent. The test asserts both txs land (`l4_entries >= 2`) and the live alice balance post = 102M. Confirm the pre-computation contract is stable.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:103:**Round-5 RQ6 type-use false-positives**: The 10 type names in TB_13_TYPE_NAMES — are any of them generic enough to false-positive in non-TB-13 code? Specifically `EventId`, `OutcomeSide`, `ShareAmount`. Grep the codebase outside FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR and confirm these names don't appear in legacy code that would get misclassified.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:266:Re-evaluate Q1-Q9 against round-5 HEAD. Also evaluate the round-5-specific risks:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:268:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. Q1 was PASS in round-3; round-5 doesn't change mint logic. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:270:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:272:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Same as Q2 above. Tests at `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs::sg_13_6` should still cover both mismatch cases.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:274:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` `total_supply_micro` + `assert_total_ctf_conserved`. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:276:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: `assert_complete_set_balanced` MIN form. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:278:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: `MarketSeedTx` accept arm. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:280:**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:282:**Q8 (underflow)**: Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:284:**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Round-5 added type-use discovery. Re-evaluate the fence's bypass surface. Specifically: with type-use walking, can a contributor still introduce a TB-13 contribution that bypasses both the marker AND type-use layers? E.g., a file that uses TB-13 types only via aliases (`use crate::state::typed_tx::CompleteSetMintTx as Foo;` then `let x: Foo = ...`) — does the type-use scan catch the `use ... as` line? It should because `CompleteSetMintTx` appears on the use-line itself, even though the bound name is `Foo`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:288:**Round-5 RQ3 smoke determinism**: The smoke pre-computes the post-mint state_root via `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx)`. If the function's hashing is somehow non-deterministic (timestamps, hashmap ordering), the redeem's parent_state_root would be wrong and the redeem would reject with StaleParent. The test asserts both txs land (`l4_entries >= 2`) and the live alice balance post = 102M. Confirm the pre-computation contract is stable.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:290:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:292:**Round-5 RQ6 type-use false-positives**: The 10 type names in TB_13_TYPE_NAMES — are any of them generic enough to false-positive in non-TB-13 code? Specifically `EventId`, `OutcomeSide`, `ShareAmount`. Grep the codebase outside FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR and confirm these names don't appear in legacy code that would get misclassified.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1179:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1180:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:68:**RQ3 — non-empty TB-13 chaintape replay smoke**: New file `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs` boots Git2LedgerWriter-backed sequencer with pre-seeded initial_q, wires real AgentKeypair, submits real signed CompleteSetMint + CompleteSetRedeem, asserts post-replay `final_state_root_hex == hex(live state_root_t)` — cryptographic proof of non-empty TB-13 map reconstruction (state_root is SHA-256 chain-fold over full QState). Evidence at `handover/evidence/tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03/`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1182:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:76:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check? Specifically: does removing the resolution_ref wrapper make TB-15+ multi-resolver evolution easier (fewer wire fields to reshape) or harder (lost a future-extension hook)?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1188:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:93:- **RQ3 smoke determinism**: pre-computes post-mint state_root via `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx)` to chain the redeem without racing the driver. Are there any QState fields that could go non-deterministic under replay (timestamps / random salts) that would invalidate the state-root equality argument?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1191:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:191:    f"**Round-5 closures**: RQ3 (chaintape replay smoke) + Q9/RQ6 (type-use fence) + RQ5 (ResolutionRef wrapper drop)\n"
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1194:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:42:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1195:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1196:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:48:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1197:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:50:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` hold after every transition? The invariant: for every event in conditional_collateral_t, `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`. The MIN form (vs strict `Σ_yes==Σ_no==collateral`) was discovered mid-development as the correct form post-redemption — losing-side shares strand above collateral. Trace the invariant body in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` and challenge the rule. Tests: `sg_13_1` (post-mint balanced; both equal), `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` (post-redeem MIN form).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1198:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:52:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance? Trace MarketSeed accept arm: `collateral_amount > 0` else `InsufficientCollateral` (SG-13.4); `balances_t[provider] >= collateral_amount` else `InsufficientBalanceForMint` (SG-13.3). Test: `sg_13_3`, `sg_13_4`, `halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1199:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:54:**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Are conditional shares anywhere counted as Coin? `total_supply_micro` must EXCLUDE `conditional_share_balances_t`. Read the function body and confirm. Architect CR-13.3 + SG-13.2 explicit.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1200:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:62:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1201:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:64:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1205:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1499:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4282:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1500:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4286:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1501:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4288:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1502:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4290:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` hold after every transition? The invariant: for every event in conditional_collateral_t, `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`. The MIN form (vs strict `Σ_yes==Σ_no==collateral`) was discovered mid-development as the correct form post-redemption — losing-side shares strand above collateral. Trace the invariant body in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` and challenge the rule. Tests: `sg_13_1` (post-mint balanced; both equal), `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` (post-redeem MIN form).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1503:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4292:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance? Trace MarketSeed accept arm: `collateral_amount > 0` else `InsufficientCollateral` (SG-13.4); `balances_t[provider] >= collateral_amount` else `InsufficientBalanceForMint` (SG-13.3). Test: `sg_13_3`, `sg_13_4`, `halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1504:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4294:**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Are conditional shares anywhere counted as Coin? `total_supply_micro` must EXCLUDE `conditional_share_balances_t`. Read the function body and confirm. Architect CR-13.3 + SG-13.2 explicit.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1505:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4302:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1506:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4304:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1510:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4333:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1523:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4517:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1525:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4707:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1628:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6592:Q1/Q4/Q6/Q7 clear for non-forged, non-negative flows: mint/seed now reject `<= 0`, move balance to collateral 1:1, and `total_supply_micro` includes `conditional_collateral_t` while omitting shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1593), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1816), [monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:172)). RQ3 smoke evidence shows `economic_state_reconstructed: true`, but it does not exercise TB-13 txs.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2370:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:8421:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2371:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:15:**Role**: skeptical adversarial implementer-reviewer for the TB-13 (CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx) ship-gate dual external audit. This is **round-4** in audit numbering (the project's session-internal "round-5" closure of RQ3 / Q9-RQ6 / RQ5 from the round-3 verdict). Independent of Gemini ship audit (parallel architectural angle).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2372:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:29:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2373:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:38:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2374:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:39:- `src/state/typed_tx.rs`: ResolutionRef struct deleted; CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; CompleteSetRedeemSigningPayload 8→7 fields. `to_signing_payload` projection drops `resolution_ref`. `InvalidResolutionRef` doc-comment + Display now read "outcome does not match state" (was "resolution_ref.claimed_outcome").
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2375:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:41:- `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`: `TxKind::CompleteSetRedeem` doc-comment fixed (was "Validates resolution_ref against L4 (must be TaskBankruptcy for No or FinalizeReward for Yes)" — drift; now correctly states resolution authority is `task_markets_t.state`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2376:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:42:- `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`: ResolutionRef import + build_redeem fixture pruned. SG-13.5 / SG-13.6 unchanged in behavior.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2377:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:44:- R-022 skip token issued for the removed ResolutionRef pub-symbol backlink. Justification at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2378:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:53:- `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs`: added `TB_13_TYPE_NAMES` constant (10 names: CompleteSetMintTx / CompleteSetRedeemTx / MarketSeedTx / ConditionalCollateralIndex / ConditionalShareBalances / ShareSidePair / EventNotOpen / EventId / OutcomeSide / ShareAmount). Refactored `discover_tb_13_files` into `discover_by_marker` (round-2 logic) ∪ `discover_by_type_use` (new). Type-use walk catches files using TB-13 types on non-comment lines even without authoring markers.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2379:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:54:- New unit test `discover_by_type_use_catches_unmarked_imports_and_skips_doc_xref` validates: (a) unmarked file using CompleteSetMintTx is caught; (b) doc-xref-only file (only /// or //! mentions) is skipped; (c) marker walk alone misses the unmarked file (proves type-use is the load-bearing layer).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2380:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:62:- New file `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs` with `rq3_non_empty_tb13_chaintape_replays_with_state_root_match`: bootstraps chain-backed sequencer with `initial_q` (alice = 100 Coin, Open task-MINT, pre-seeded Finalized task-REDEEM with shares + collateral), wires real AgentKeypair via `AgentKeypairRegistry::open` (writes agent_pubkeys.json) + `set_agent_pubkeys`, submits real signed CompleteSetMintTx + CompleteSetRedeemTx through `submit_agent_tx` → driver → Git2LedgerWriter. Holds `Arc<Sequencer>` clone across `bundle.shutdown().await` to read post-drain live `q_snapshot`. Asserts:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2381:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:71:- The two-tx chain (initial → mint → redeem) replays deterministically end-to-end via `verify_chaintape` Gate 4 covering both `CompleteSetMint` + `CompleteSetRedeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2382:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:72:- `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root` pre-computation is sound and matches the dispatcher's apply-time computation (no StaleParent rejection).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2383:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:79:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. Q1 was PASS in round-3; round-5 doesn't change mint logic. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2384:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2385:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:85:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` `total_supply_micro` + `assert_total_ctf_conserved`. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2386:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:87:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: `assert_complete_set_balanced` MIN form. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2387:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:89:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: `MarketSeedTx` accept arm. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2388:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:95:**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Round-5 added type-use discovery. Re-evaluate the fence's bypass surface. Specifically: with type-use walking, can a contributor still introduce a TB-13 contribution that bypasses both the marker AND type-use layers? E.g., a file that uses TB-13 types only via aliases (`use crate::state::typed_tx::CompleteSetMintTx as Foo;` then `let x: Foo = ...`) — does the type-use scan catch the `use ... as` line? It should because `CompleteSetMintTx` appears on the use-line itself, even though the bound name is `Foo`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2389:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:99:**Round-5 RQ3 smoke determinism**: The smoke pre-computes the post-mint state_root via `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx)`. If the function's hashing is somehow non-deterministic (timestamps, hashmap ordering), the redeem's parent_state_root would be wrong and the redeem would reject with StaleParent. The test asserts both txs land (`l4_entries >= 2`) and the live alice balance post = 102M. Confirm the pre-computation contract is stable.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2390:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2391:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:105:**Round-5 R-022 skip-token**: ResolutionRef pub struct removed; the R-022 hook caught the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink. The skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` justifies the removal. Confirm the OBS doc is sufficient: does it explain why no replacement TRACE_MATRIX entry is needed?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2432:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1086:   197	    // appear to burn money, failing assert_total_ctf_conserved with empty
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2438:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:44:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2439:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:48:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2440:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:50:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2441:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:52:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` hold after every transition? The invariant: for every event in conditional_collateral_t, `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`. The MIN form (vs strict `Σ_yes==Σ_no==collateral`) was discovered mid-development as the correct form post-redemption — losing-side shares strand above collateral. Trace the invariant body in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` and challenge the rule. Tests: `sg_13_1` (post-mint balanced; both equal), `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` (post-redeem MIN form).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2442:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:54:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance? Trace MarketSeed accept arm: `collateral_amount > 0` else `InsufficientCollateral` (SG-13.4); `balances_t[provider] >= collateral_amount` else `InsufficientBalanceForMint` (SG-13.3). Test: `sg_13_3`, `sg_13_4`, `halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2443:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:56:**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Are conditional shares anywhere counted as Coin? `total_supply_micro` must EXCLUDE `conditional_share_balances_t`. Read the function body and confirm. Architect CR-13.3 + SG-13.2 explicit.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2444:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:64:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2445:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:66:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2449:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2456:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:21:**Role**: skeptical adversarial implementer-reviewer for the TB-13 (CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx) ship-gate dual external audit. This is **round-4** in audit numbering (the project's session-internal "round-5" closure of RQ3 / Q9-RQ6 / RQ5 from the round-3 verdict). Independent of Gemini ship audit (parallel architectural angle).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2457:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:35:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2458:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:44:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2468:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:85:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. Q1 was PASS in round-3; round-5 doesn't change mint logic. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2469:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:87:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2470:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:91:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` `total_supply_micro` + `assert_total_ctf_conserved`. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2471:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:93:**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: `assert_complete_set_balanced` MIN form. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2472:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:95:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: `MarketSeedTx` accept arm. Round-5 doesn't touch this. Confirm.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2473:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:101:**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Round-5 added type-use discovery. Re-evaluate the fence's bypass surface. Specifically: with type-use walking, can a contributor still introduce a TB-13 contribution that bypasses both the marker AND type-use layers? E.g., a file that uses TB-13 types only via aliases (`use crate::state::typed_tx::CompleteSetMintTx as Foo;` then `let x: Foo = ...`) — does the type-use scan catch the `use ... as` line? It should because `CompleteSetMintTx` appears on the use-line itself, even though the bound name is `Foo`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2474:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:105:**Round-5 RQ3 smoke determinism**: The smoke pre-computes the post-mint state_root via `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx)`. If the function's hashing is somehow non-deterministic (timestamps, hashmap ordering), the redeem's parent_state_root would be wrong and the redeem would reject with StaleParent. The test asserts both txs land (`l4_entries >= 2`) and the live alice balance post = 102M. Confirm the pre-computation contract is stable.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2475:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:107:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2478:handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_TB_1_VERDICT_2026-04-29.md:71:- **P1-G2**: `assert_total_ctf_conserved`'s `exempt_tx_kinds` slice is forward-thinking and won't need rewriting at P3 RSP-4 / P8.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2479:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:34:**Q4. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` call shape**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2486:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:82:-   **CTF conservation failure**: Impossible. As established in Q1 and Q4, `NodePosition` is not a monetary holding. The only economic mutations are balanced transfers, and `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called in every accept arm (`src/state/sequencer.rs:601`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:748`, etc.).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2487:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:84:-   **NodePosition counted as Coin**: Impossible. See Q4. The `total_supply_micro` function (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152`) is the sole authority and does not include `node_positions_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2488:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:1:# Gemini TB-13 Ship Audit — CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx (Class 3)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2489:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:6:**Round-5 closures**: RQ3 (chaintape replay smoke) + Q9/RQ6 (type-use fence) + RQ5 (ResolutionRef wrapper drop)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2490:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:24:3.  The removal of the `ResolutionRef` wrapper (RQ5) simplifies the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` wire format and, critically, resolves the architectural concern I raised in round-3 regarding future extensibility. The new design, which couples redemption to the canonical `TaskMarketState` enum rather than a specific transaction type, is cleaner and more forward-compatible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2491:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:34:-   **RQ3 smoke determinism**: **PASS**. The new test at `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs` correctly proves non-empty replay determinism. The state-root equality argument holds. The test uses `timestamp_logical` for transaction ordering, which is a deterministic counter, not a non-deterministic wall-clock time. The `QState` struct and its sub-fields do not contain any other sources of non-determinism (e.g., random salts) that would invalidate the state-root comparison. The use of `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root` to pre-compute the parent for the subsequent redeem transaction is a sound method for creating a deterministic, multi-step chaintape for testing purposes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2492:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:36:-   **RQ5 wire-format break**: **PASS**. The removal of `ResolutionRef` from `CompleteSetRedeemTx` at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1178` constitutes a wire-format break. As TB-13 has not shipped and has no production data, this is acceptable and desirable. The accompanying skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` provides a correct and sufficient justification, noting that the removed fields were either unused (`resolution_tx_id`) or redundant (`claimed_outcome`). No persisted artifacts encoding the old shape were found in the codebase.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2493:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:40:-   **R-022 skip-token**: **PASS**. The justification in `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` is sound. The `ResolutionRef` struct was correctly identified as carrying dead weight. Its role is fully and more cleanly absorbed by the `outcome` field on `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself, which is validated directly against the canonical `TaskMarketState`. The TRACE_MATRIX backlink is correctly retired as the symbol it pointed to is gone.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2494:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:46:1.  **Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2495:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:47:    **Finding**: **PASS**. No. The logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1808` remains a pure balance↔collateral migration. `balances_t` is debited and `conditional_collateral_t` is credited by the same amount. The 6-holding sum defined in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:158` correctly includes `conditional_collateral_t`, ensuring `assert_total_ctf_conserved` passes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2496:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:53:    **Finding**: **PASS**. No. The `ResolutionRef` wrapper has been removed, simplifying the check. The `match` arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889` now directly compares `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` tuple correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. The logic is sound and more direct than before.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2497:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:55:4.  **Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2498:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:56:    **Finding**: **PASS**. Yes. All three new transaction types represent balanced transfers between coin holdings (balances ↔ collateral). The 6-holding sum in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:158` is correct, and the logic is verified by integration tests like `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2499:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:58:5.  **Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` (MIN-semantics) hold after every transition?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2500:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:61:6.  **Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2501:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:65:    **Finding**: **PASS**. No. `total_supply_micro` at `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:158` correctly excludes `conditional_share_balances_t` from the sum, per architect CR-13.3.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2502:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:83:12. **With ResolutionRef now removed, is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2503:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:84:    **Finding**: **PASS (Addressed)**. Yes, the concern is fully and elegantly addressed. My previous challenge noted that coupling redemption to specific transaction types via `ResolutionRef` was brittle. By removing `ResolutionRef` and having the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` check the canonical `task_markets_t.state` enum directly, the design is now much more extensible. If a future TB introduces a new resolution mechanism (e.g., a `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`), it only needs to mutate `task_markets_t.state` to a resolved value. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` logic will not need to be changed at all. This is a significant architectural improvement.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2504:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:86:13. **Is the MIN-semantics `assert_complete_set_balanced` invariant the right form?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2505:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:97:**Rationale**: The TB-13 implementation is now in an excellent state. The round-5 fixes have not only closed the specific challenges raised but have resulted in a simpler, more robust, and more extensible design. The addition of cryptographic proof for non-empty replay determinism provides strong assurance. My primary architectural concern from the previous round has been fully resolved by the removal of `ResolutionRef`. There are no remaining VETO or CHALLENGE-level issues.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2548:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:42:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2552:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:52:**Q6 (seed solvency)**: Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance? Trace MarketSeed accept arm: `collateral_amount > 0` else `InsufficientCollateral` (SG-13.4); `balances_t[provider] >= collateral_amount` else `InsufficientBalanceForMint` (SG-13.3). Test: `sg_13_3`, `sg_13_4`, `halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2553:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:54:**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Are conditional shares anywhere counted as Coin? `total_supply_micro` must EXCLUDE `conditional_share_balances_t`. Read the function body and confirm. Architect CR-13.3 + SG-13.2 explicit.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2554:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:62:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2555:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:64:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2559:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2565:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:288:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:84:-   **NodePosition counted as Coin**: Impossible. See Q4. The `total_supply_micro` function (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152`) is the sole authority and does not include `node_positions_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2576:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:486:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_12_2026-05-03.md:44:| **4-holding CTF (post-TB-8)** [doc-corrected post-Codex CHALLENGE Q4] | NodePosition is NOT a holding term per CR-12.1 + CR-12.2. Σ holdings = balances + escrows + stakes + challenge_cases (claims-active was removed from `total_supply_micro` in TB-8 — per `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:113` "Counted ... 4 holdings post-TB-8"). NodePosition addition keeps the count at 4. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2611:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:529:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:243:Gemini also raised one non-blocking CHALLENGE on Q12 (`ResolutionRef`
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2626:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:557:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:32:*   **Finding**: The transaction is a pure migration of value from one Coin-holding ledger (`balances_t`) to another (`conditional_collateral_t`). The total supply of Coin is bit-for-bit conserved. The subsequent minting of `conditional_share_balances_t` does not affect Coin supply, as shares are correctly excluded from the `total_supply_micro` sum (see Q7).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2693:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1463:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:92:The core economic invariants (Q1-Q9) are soundly implemented and rigorously tested. The architecture is robust and forward-compatible for its intended purpose (Q10-Q12). However, the lack of a state check in the `CompleteSetMintTx` and `MarketSeedTx` dispatch arms (Q13) represents a minor but tangible architectural smell that should be rectified before shipping. It does not violate a halting trigger but fails the principle of least surprise and could complicate future layers that build on this substrate. The fix is localized and low-risk.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2737:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1802:handover/ai-direct/TB-5_RSP3_RESOLUTION_GATE_2026-04-30.md:969:| `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` | **MINIMAL CASCADE per Q4 amendment**: +ChallengeResolve arm in `assert_no_post_init_mint` exhaustive match (line 214-227) + K5-test fixture update (line 348-356). 5-holding count + total_supply_micro UNCHANGED. | `git diff main..HEAD -- src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs \| wc -l` ≤ 30 net add |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2885:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:2204:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:767:| Q7 | Resolution reference shape | `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }`; references TaskBankruptcyTx (NO) or FinalizeRewardTx (YES); validated via L4 lookup + outcome match in sequencer. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2886:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:2205:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:769:| Q9 | Halting triggers | total_supply_micro mutation incorrect, shares counted as Coin, MarketSeed without balance, legacy CPMM import in TB-13, f64 in new modules, any AMM/CPMM router introduced. MUST stop immediately. |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:2973:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:2385:handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:39:# 2026-05-03 TB-12 SHIPPED — "P3 carry-forward + P4 primary: Node Exposure Index per architect 2026-05-03 ruling §3 + §10". Class 3 envelope (Atom 1 = Class 2 schema; Atom 2 = Class 3 dispatch wire; Atom 6 = Class 3 dual audit Codex + Gemini both ✓). Self-audit PASS; Codex CHALLENGE × 2 (Q4 doc-drift on 4-vs-5-holding total_supply; Q5 legacy CPMM in src/prediction_market.rs predates TB-12) — both resolved as documentation/scope clarifications via recursive audit §10 + OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE_2026-05-03.md (TB-13 prerequisite); Gemini round-1 PASS / high conviction / PROCEED to SHIP (gemini-2.5-pro, 896k char prompt, 48.2s API; 8/8 audit questions PASS). User-architect ultrathink verified architect §9 strict alignment before SHIP — found 4 SG-12.x test-name drifts (SG-12.5 / 12.6 / 12.7 / 12.8 names didn't exactly match architect's "passes" strings); all 4 fixed BEFORE SHIP per feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite (NOT post-ship patch): SG-12.5 renamed to sg_12_5_replay_reconstructs_node_positions; SG-12.6 ADDED sg_12_6_dashboard_view_positions_works inside src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tb12_render_tests with 4 cases + forbidden-token grep; SG-12.7 renamed to sg_12_7_no_market_trading_variants_introduced; SG-12.8 ADDED sg_12_8_no_node_market_entry_as_canonical_state at integration-test level (q_state.rs unit alias kept as defense-in-depth). 8/8 SG-12.x by architect §9.3 EXACT names PASS. cargo test --workspace = 759/0/150 (+12 net vs TB-11 ship 747; +28 vs TB-10 ship 731). Architectural deltas: NEW src/state/typed_tx.rs (NodePosition struct + PositionSide enum + PositionKind enum); EXTEND src/state/q_state.rs (NodePositionsIndex flat shape + EconomicState 10→11 sub-fields with +node_positions_t serde-default); EXTEND src/state/sequencer.rs (WorkTx + ChallengeTx accept-arm pure-additive NodePosition write — no balances_t / stakes_t / challenge_cases_t / total_supply mutation; CR-12.1 + CR-12.2 verified); EXTEND src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs (NodePosition NOT in 4-holding total_supply_micro; structural — assert_total_ctf_conserved unchanged); EXTEND src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs (§13 Node exposure records + render_section_13 pure-fn helper for SG-12.6 unit-testability + LABEL DISCIPLINE "exposure records" NOT "Open market balances" per architect §8 Atom 4); EXTEND experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs (TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward — 4 EvidenceCapsule counters + write_evidence_capsule + tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run wired at MAX_TX exhausted exit point); EXTEND experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/lean_market.rs (3 new subcommands: tick POLICY PREVIEW MODE — actual emission deferred to system_keypair persistence + view-bankruptcy read-only listing + view-positions read-only NodePosition browser); REHASH genesis_payload.toml trust_root for src/state/sequencer.rs + src/state/typed_tx.rs + src/state/q_state.rs + src/state/mod.rs + src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs + experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs (lean_market.rs outside trust root by TB-10 precedent). Halting triggers (architect §7) NONE triggered: CTF conservation passes (Gemini Q1+Q4); position-tx field derivation exact (position_fields_derived_from_source_tx_exactly test PASS); NodePosition NOT counted as Coin (4-holding model unchanged); replay deterministic (sg_12_5 PASS); no Codex/Gemini VETO. Production claim: "TB-12 establishes a flat, immutable, non-monetary NodePositionsIndex anchoring WorkTx.stake → FirstLong + ChallengeTx.stake → ChallengeShort exposure records on canonical L4-derived QState. NodePosition is exposure record NOT active position balance per architect §10 critical insight; TB-13 CompleteSet introduces real YES/NO claims; TB-14 PriceIndex computes price from long/short interest; TB-16 controlled-arena introduces P&L. Charter Q6 (ii.5) sync mode honored — dual audit complete + user-architect ultrathink verdict before SHIP." Open follow-ups (carry-forward, NOT ship blockers): TB-13 prerequisite quarantine of src/prediction_market.rs legacy f64 CPMM (OBS-tracked); system_keypair persistence + reattachment factory for lean_market tick on-chain emission; real-LLM zeta-regularization rerun (Atom 0.5(a) wired the call site; manual user-driven session post-ship).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3404:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:7767:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:42:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3416:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:7813:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4707:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3461:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8084:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6608:Q1/Q4/Q6/Q7 clear for non-forged, non-negative flows: mint/seed now reject `<= 0`, move balance to collateral 1:1, and `total_supply_micro` includes `conditional_collateral_t` while omitting shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1593), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1816), [monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:172)). Q2/Q3/RQ1 clear on state/outcome gates ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1678), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1691)). RQ3 smoke evidence shows `economic_state_reconstructed: true`, but it does not exercise TB-13 txs.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3464:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8358:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:42:**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3465:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8360:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:62:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3542:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8643:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:8407:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.

A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3547:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8775:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:62:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3566:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9402:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:77:**Finding**: PASS. Yes. The MIN-semantics invariant is correct and more robust than a strict equality check on both sides. It correctly models the post-resolution state where losing-side shares become unbacked claims. My primary adversarial concern was minting/seeding into an already-resolved event to grief the chain state. The recursive self-audit's remediation log (`RECURSIVE_AUDIT...md` §12.3, Gemini Q13 fix) confirms that an `EventNotOpen` gate was added to the `CompleteSetMintTx` and `MarketSeedTx` dispatch arms at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1823` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:1959` respectively. This closes the attack vector and makes the overall design robust.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3567:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10017:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:19:**Q6 — PASS.** `assert_total_ctf_conserved` appears in `sequencer.rs` only as an import at `src/state/sequencer.rs:38` and one production call at `src/state/sequencer.rs:229`, and that call passes `&[]` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:232`. This satisfies preflight red line 4 at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:554`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3576:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10203:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:11:**Q3 [CHALLENGE].** The accepted path is already on `transition_ledger`/`LedgerWriter`: `apply_one` canonical-encodes the `TypedTx`, CAS-puts it, builds `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, signs it, folds `ledger_root`, commits via `writer_w.commit(&entry)`, stores `next_logical_t`, writes `q_next`, and sets `q_w.ledger_root_t` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:351`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:377`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:386`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:423`). The WorkTx shape mostly supports the validation inputs: it has `task_id`, `parent_state_root`, `agent_id`, `predicate_results`, and `stake` (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:223`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:235`), and `TypedTx` has `tx_kind()` plus `HasSubmitter::submitter_id()` rather than a `submitter_id` field (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:620`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:631`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:641`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:698`). The cascade risk is real: `EconomicState` exposes `escrows_t` and `task_markets_t`, but both are keyed by `TxId`, while `WorkTx.task_id` is a `TaskId` (`src/state/q_state.rs:143`, `src/state/q_state.rs:149`, `src/state/q_state.rs:161`, `src/state/q_state.rs:224`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:33`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:35`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:225`). A task-keyed `EscrowVault` exists, but it is separate from `QState` and explicitly "distinct from `state::q_state::EscrowEntry`" (`src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:15`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:51`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:53`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:146`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:168`). `assert_total_ctf_conserved(..., &[])` is callable, but non-empty exemptions short-circuit (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:167`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:179`). `RejectionEvidenceWriter::append_rejected` has the named fields and is callable (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:268`), but `Sequencer` currently has no rejection writer field or accessor (`src/state/sequencer.rs:230`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:247`). Missing method/field finding: the preflight must specify how L4.E writer ownership and task-id-to-economic-index lookup work before Phase-1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3581:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10786:handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:39:# 2026-05-03 TB-12 SHIPPED — "P3 carry-forward + P4 primary: Node Exposure Index per architect 2026-05-03 ruling §3 + §10". Class 3 envelope (Atom 1 = Class 2 schema; Atom 2 = Class 3 dispatch wire; Atom 6 = Class 3 dual audit Codex + Gemini both ✓). Self-audit PASS; Codex CHALLENGE × 2 (Q4 doc-drift on 4-vs-5-holding total_supply; Q5 legacy CPMM in src/prediction_market.rs predates TB-12) — both resolved as documentation/scope clarifications via recursive audit §10 + OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE_2026-05-03.md (TB-13 prerequisite); Gemini round-1 PASS / high conviction / PROCEED to SHIP (gemini-2.5-pro, 896k char prompt, 48.2s API; 8/8 audit questions PASS). User-architect ultrathink verified architect §9 strict alignment before SHIP — found 4 SG-12.x test-name drifts (SG-12.5 / 12.6 / 12.7 / 12.8 names didn't exactly match architect's "passes" strings); all 4 fixed BEFORE SHIP per feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite (NOT post-ship patch): SG-12.5 renamed to sg_12_5_replay_reconstructs_node_positions; SG-12.6 ADDED sg_12_6_dashboard_view_positions_works inside src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tb12_render_tests with 4 cases + forbidden-token grep; SG-12.7 renamed to sg_12_7_no_market_trading_variants_introduced; SG-12.8 ADDED sg_12_8_no_node_market_entry_as_canonical_state at integration-test level (q_state.rs unit alias kept as defense-in-depth). 8/8 SG-12.x by architect §9.3 EXACT names PASS. cargo test --workspace = 759/0/150 (+12 net vs TB-11 ship 747; +28 vs TB-10 ship 731). Architectural deltas: NEW src/state/typed_tx.rs (NodePosition struct + PositionSide enum + PositionKind enum); EXTEND src/state/q_state.rs (NodePositionsIndex flat shape + EconomicState 10→11 sub-fields with +node_positions_t serde-default); EXTEND src/state/sequencer.rs (WorkTx + ChallengeTx accept-arm pure-additive NodePosition write — no balances_t / stakes_t / challenge_cases_t / total_supply mutation; CR-12.1 + CR-12.2 verified); EXTEND src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs (NodePosition NOT in 4-holding total_supply_micro; structural — assert_total_ctf_conserved unchanged); EXTEND src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs (§13 Node exposure records + render_section_13 pure-fn helper for SG-12.6 unit-testability + LABEL DISCIPLINE "exposure records" NOT "Open market balances" per architect §8 Atom 4); EXTEND experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs (TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward — 4 EvidenceCapsule counters + write_evidence_capsule + tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run wired at MAX_TX exhausted exit point); EXTEND experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/lean_market.rs (3 new subcommands: tick POLICY PREVIEW MODE — actual emission deferred to system_keypair persistence + view-bankruptcy read-only listing + view-positions read-only NodePosition browser); REHASH genesis_payload.toml trust_root for src/state/sequencer.rs + src/state/typed_tx.rs + src/state/q_state.rs + src/state/mod.rs + src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs + experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs (lean_market.rs outside trust root by TB-10 precedent). Halting triggers (architect §7) NONE triggered: CTF conservation passes (Gemini Q1+Q4); position-tx field derivation exact (position_fields_derived_from_source_tx_exactly test PASS); NodePosition NOT counted as Coin (4-holding model unchanged); replay deterministic (sg_12_5 PASS); no Codex/Gemini VETO. Production claim: "TB-12 establishes a flat, immutable, non-monetary NodePositionsIndex anchoring WorkTx.stake → FirstLong + ChallengeTx.stake → ChallengeShort exposure records on canonical L4-derived QState. NodePosition is exposure record NOT active position balance per architect §10 critical insight; TB-13 CompleteSet introduces real YES/NO claims; TB-14 PriceIndex computes price from long/short interest; TB-16 controlled-arena introduces P&L. Charter Q6 (ii.5) sync mode honored — dual audit complete + user-architect ultrathink verdict before SHIP." Open follow-ups (carry-forward, NOT ship blockers): TB-13 prerequisite quarantine of src/prediction_market.rs legacy f64 CPMM (OBS-tracked); system_keypair persistence + reattachment factory for lean_market tick on-chain emission; real-LLM zeta-regularization rerun (Atom 0.5(a) wired the call site; manual user-driven session post-ship).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3590:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11057:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:769:| Q9 | Halting triggers | total_supply_micro mutation incorrect, shares counted as Coin, MarketSeed without balance, legacy CPMM import in TB-13, f64 in new modules, any AMM/CPMM router introduced. MUST stop immediately. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3592:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11089:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:48:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3593:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11091:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:64:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:3594:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11092:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
