handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7069:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3776:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12746:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7070:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3780:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13078:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:27:However, the audit identifies one non-blocking but significant architectural risk concerning the forward-compatibility of the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12), which warrants a **CHALLENGE**. The current model tightly couples redemption to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`), which may prove brittle in TB-15+ multi-resolver scenarios. While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7071:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3782:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13082:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7072:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3806:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:25:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7073:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3815:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:66:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7074:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3816:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:67:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7075:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3822:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:118:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7076:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3825:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:136:| G9 TransitionError variants: 5 NEW additive                       | ✓ pass     | InsufficientBalanceForMint / RedeemBeforeResolution / RedeemMoreThanOwned / InsufficientCollateral / InvalidResolutionRef. |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7081:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3869:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:*   Any other combination on a resolved market, such as `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)`, falls through to the wildcard arm which returns `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`.

The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7084:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3886:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7085:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4001:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:27:However, the audit identifies one non-blocking but significant architectural risk concerning the forward-compatibility of the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12), which warrants a **CHALLENGE**. The current model tightly couples redemption to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`), which may prove brittle in TB-15+ multi-resolver scenarios. While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7086:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4005:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:43:**Finding**: PASS. No. The dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889-1899` contains an explicit `match` on `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` pair correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7087:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4013:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:73:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7088:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4014:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:74:**Finding**: CHALLENGE. The current `ResolutionRef` model at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1120` and its validation logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` are tightly coupled to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). If TB-15+ introduces new resolution mechanisms (e.g., `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`, `OracleUpdateTx`), the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm will require modification to recognize these new transaction types. A more abstract and robust long-term design might involve a canonical `ResolutionsIndex` in `QState` that maps `EventId` to a resolved `OutcomeSide`, decoupling the redemption logic from the specific mechanism of resolution. The current implementation is not a bug but represents an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7089:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4017:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7090:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4028:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7091:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4037:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:72:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7092:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4038:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7093:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4070:handover/alignment/OBS_RESOLUTIONS_INDEX_TB15_2026-05-03.md:12:TB-13's `ResolutionRef` model couples redemption logic to specific

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7096:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4078:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:1:# OBS R-022 — TB-13 ResolutionRef removed (TRACE_MATRIX backlink retired)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7097:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4079:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:5:**Removed symbol**: `pub struct ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7098:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4081:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:10:ResolutionRef carried two dead fields:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7099:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4082:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:13:- `claimed_outcome: OutcomeSide` — a redundant copy of `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`. Used only by an inner-consistency gate `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome → InvalidResolutionRef`. That gate was dead defense-in-depth: the agent signature covers both fields, so any tampering that desynchronized them would break the signature first.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7100:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4083:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:19:The TB-13 typed-tx surface is still flowchart-anchored — `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself retains its `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3 + FR-13.4..5 + SG-13.5..6)` backlink at `src/state/typed_tx.rs`. ResolutionRef was an internal helper type without an independent flowchart role. Removing the type cleanly removes the backlink — there is no orphan code; the role it carried (claimed-outcome assertion) is now expressed directly via `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7101:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4084:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:25:- SG-13.6 (`sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`): PASS unchanged — including both mismatch checks (Finalized + outcome=No → `InvalidResolutionRef`; Bankrupt + outcome=Yes → `InvalidResolutionRef`). The match arm using `(market_state, redeem.outcome)` produces identical rejection behavior to the previous `(market_state, claimed_outcome)` form because all fixtures set `claimed_outcome = outcome` (the inner-consistency check could never independently fire on an honestly-signed tx).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7122:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:35:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7123:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:44:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7130:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:87:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7131:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:107:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7133:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:6:**Round-5 closures**: RQ3 (chaintape replay smoke) + Q9/RQ6 (type-use fence) + RQ5 (ResolutionRef wrapper drop)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7134:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:24:3.  The removal of the `ResolutionRef` wrapper (RQ5) simplifies the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` wire format and, critically, resolves the architectural concern I raised in round-3 regarding future extensibility. The new design, which couples redemption to the canonical `TaskMarketState` enum rather than a specific transaction type, is cleaner and more forward-compatible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7135:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:36:-   **RQ5 wire-format break**: **PASS**. The removal of `ResolutionRef` from `CompleteSetRedeemTx` at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1178` constitutes a wire-format break. As TB-13 has not shipped and has no production data, this is acceptable and desirable. The accompanying skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` provides a correct and sufficient justification, noting that the removed fields were either unused (`resolution_tx_id`) or redundant (`claimed_outcome`). No persisted artifacts encoding the old shape were found in the codebase.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7136:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:40:-   **R-022 skip-token**: **PASS**. The justification in `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` is sound. The `ResolutionRef` struct was correctly identified as carrying dead weight. Its role is fully and more cleanly absorbed by the `outcome` field on `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself, which is validated directly against the canonical `TaskMarketState`. The TRACE_MATRIX backlink is correctly retired as the symbol it pointed to is gone.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7137:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:53:    **Finding**: **PASS**. No. The `ResolutionRef` wrapper has been removed, simplifying the check. The `match` arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889` now directly compares `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` tuple correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. The logic is sound and more direct than before.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7138:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:83:12. **With ResolutionRef now removed, is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7139:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:84:    **Finding**: **PASS (Addressed)**. Yes, the concern is fully and elegantly addressed. My previous challenge noted that coupling redemption to specific transaction types via `ResolutionRef` was brittle. By removing `ResolutionRef` and having the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` check the canonical `task_markets_t.state` enum directly, the design is now much more extensible. If a future TB introduces a new resolution mechanism (e.g., a `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`), it only needs to mutate `task_markets_t.state` to a resolved value. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` logic will not need to be changed at all. This is a significant architectural improvement.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7140:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:97:**Rationale**: The TB-13 implementation is now in an excellent state. The round-5 fixes have not only closed the specific challenges raised but have resulted in a simpler, more robust, and more extensible design. The addition of cryptographic proof for non-empty replay determinism provides strong assurance. My primary architectural concern from the previous round has been fully resolved by the removal of `ResolutionRef`. There are no remaining VETO or CHALLENGE-level issues.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7141:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7142:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:62:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7143:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7234:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8775:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:62:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7242:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11092:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7244:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11385:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7249:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11916:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7251:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12343:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7254:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13078:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:27:However, the audit identifies one non-blocking but significant architectural risk concerning the forward-compatibility of the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12), which warrants a **CHALLENGE**. The current model tightly couples redemption to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`), which may prove brittle in TB-15+ multi-resolver scenarios. While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7255:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13082:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:7256:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13876:- TB13-RQ5 CHALLENGE: `resolution_tx_id` is not validated, which is not a money exploit because `task_markets_t.state` is the live truth, but source docs still promise L4 resolution lookup. Either validate it or explicitly document it as opaque/non-authoritative. Refs: [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1705), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1124), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1163), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:86).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:10424:One important issue is emerging in the RQ3 evidence: the TB-13 accept root mutators hash `(domain, previous state_root, canonical tx)`, not the full `QState`. That means `final_state_root_hex == live state_root_t` proves transaction-chain determinism, but it is not by itself cryptographic evidence that every TB-13 map byte reconstructed equal to live state.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:11895:    15	Codex round-3 RQ5 raised this as the contract drift between doc and code. The previous session's round-4 closure was a doc-only fix that left the dead wire field locked into the on-disk encoding. handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12071:    75	### TB13-RQ7 — STEP_B process artifact missing for sequencer.rs Class 3 change
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12092:    96	### Gemini Q12 — ResolutionsIndex for TB-15+
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12145:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12146:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12179:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4062:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12195:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:*   **(Q4.a) Round-trip preserved**: **PASS**. All new fields in `PputResult` are `Option<T>` and use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` (`evaluator.rs:82-93`). This is the canonical and correct way to ensure that deserializing old data and re-serializing it produces identical output.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12196:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R3_2026-04-29.md:60:- **B4a `#[serde(skip)]` conditional**: § 2.1 shows `#[serde(skip)]` on the new `TuringBus.sequencer` field unconditionally, but `src/bus.rs:53` `pub struct TuringBus` has no `Serialize/Deserialize` derive currently — the literal `#[serde(skip)]` would not be invalid syntactically (serde-skip without serde just no-ops) but is misleading documentation. v1.1 spec already has a comment "applied if TuringBus has Serialize/Deserialize" (line ~167); Codex flags that this conditional should be more explicit at the code-comment level.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12198:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:62:| Q3.d: silent skip in compute_p0 | CHALLENGE | 15b87fb: strict-completeness — fails loudly on missing tags / duplicates / seed mismatch / N≠144 |

E.g., evaluator emits PPUT_RESULT but the runner's grep fails; runner's python3 enrichment crashes; etc. Trace runner.sh:174-178 (the success path) for any silent skip.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12203:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:55:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12204:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Trust Root rehashed for typed_tx / sequencer / transition_ledger. R-022 skip token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12205:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:66:**Q9/RQ6 — type-use forward-fence discovery**: `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` now has `TB_13_TYPE_NAMES` + `discover_by_type_use` walking src/ for non-comment uses of TB-13 type names. Catches contributors who import TB-13 types without authoring markers. New unit test exercises caught/skipped/neutral cases.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12206:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:76:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12285:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:328:3. (Codex R4#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring still says "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" — contradicts H6's fail-closed runtime.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12287:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:596:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12317:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5622:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12318:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5623:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12328:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6851:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12336:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9303:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12338:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9983:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:80:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12339:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9989:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:117:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12345:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10643:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round3_audit.py:65:| Q3.d: silent skip in compute_p0 | CHALLENGE | 15b87fb: strict-completeness — fails loudly on missing tags / duplicates / seed mismatch / N≠144 |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12346:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10668:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:79:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12347:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10677:handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:116:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12348:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10709:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:62:| Q3.d: silent skip in compute_p0 | CHALLENGE | 15b87fb: strict-completeness — fails loudly on missing tags / duplicates / seed mismatch / N≠144 |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12363:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12320:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3784:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:45:-   **(Q3.d) Silent drop in `compute_p0.py`**: **CHALLENGE**. The script at `compute_p0.py:56-58` silently skips rows that are missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed`. A failure in the runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh:170-177`) to stamp a row would cause that data point to be silently dropped, biasing the `p_0` result without warning. The script should fail loudly if any row from either input file is missing the required keys.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12424:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:14849:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:86:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12425:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:14858:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:123:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?

Does this artificially raise p_0?

If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12432:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:15023:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3769:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:116:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12433:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:15033:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3784:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:45:-   **(Q3.d) Silent drop in `compute_p0.py`**: **CHALLENGE**. The script at `compute_p0.py:56-58` silently skips rows that are missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed`. A failure in the runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh:170-177`) to stamp a row would cause that data point to be silently dropped, biasing the `p_0` result without warning. The script should fail loudly if any row from either input file is missing the required keys.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12434:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:15048:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3816:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:86:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12444:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.sh:66:- (Q3.c) `verify_post_hoc(oracle, payload)` is defined but NOT called by current evaluator. Is this dead code, or correctly deferred to Phase C? If deferred, document the contract clearly enough that Phase C can't accidentally skip it.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12445:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.sh:73:- (Q4.a) All new fields use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. A pre-B2 binary's emit produces None for new fields; deserializing on the new code path → Ok(...) with None. Round-trip preserved?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12450:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:71:## Q4 B4 closure (serde-skip conditional + LoC sync)

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12475:handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:14:| Codex | **CHALLENGE** | High | (1) sequencer logical_t skip race; (2) signing payload binds epoch + parent_ledger_root; (3) replay two-mode + CAS cold-replay |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12476:handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:36:| **K1** | **Sequencer logical_t skip race** — `fetch_add` before accept skips on rejection; skeleton + replay reject gaps | Q-C top-1 | Either (a) separate counters for submissions vs accepted entries (assign logical_t at COMMIT time, not submit), or (b) rejection entries become first-class L4 entries with logical_t. Recommend (a) for v4 minimalism. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12477:handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:115:**Recommended**: **A**. Both auditors high-conviction CHALLENGE = real defects, not principle. v1.1 is a single concentrated patch round; skipping items will cost more in round-3+.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12480:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12481:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12533:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:183:- (Q1.c) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail closed when `python3` is missing? Verify by reading the test + spot-checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12535:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:331:3. (Codex R4#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring still says "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" — contradicts H6's fail-closed runtime.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12562:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5275:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12563:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5276:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12654:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:6196:   140	- (Q1.c) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail closed when `python3` is missing? Verify by reading the test source (appended to this packet) + checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12670:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:48:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12671:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12752:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:35:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12753:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:44:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12758:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:87:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12759:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:107:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12765:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R4_2026-04-29.md:23:| B4 (serde-skip conditional + LoC sync) | ✅ PASS — § 2.1 lines 175-182 conditional comment matches source state; LoC synced 210-300 | ✅ PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12766:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12767:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:101:- Codex R3: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 narrow findings (A8 packet line 118 still calls substitution "conservative"; packet § 3 A6 atom + Q4.a still say "FC1-N12 only in oneshot" + Q4.d still describes ms drift; G1 wrapper soft-skips on missing python3).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12820:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:42:- `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`: ResolutionRef import + build_redeem fixture pruned. SG-13.5 / SG-13.6 unchanged in behavior.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12821:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:44:- R-022 skip token issued for the removed ResolutionRef pub-symbol backlink. Justification at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12822:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:54:- New unit test `discover_by_type_use_catches_unmarked_imports_and_skips_doc_xref` validates: (a) unmarked file using CompleteSetMintTx is caught; (b) doc-xref-only file (only /// or //! mentions) is skipped; (c) marker walk alone misses the unmarked file (proves type-use is the load-bearing layer).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12823:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12824:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12825:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:105:**Round-5 R-022 skip-token**: ResolutionRef pub struct removed; the R-022 hook caught the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink. The skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` justifies the removal. Confirm the OBS doc is sufficient: does it explain why no replacement TRACE_MATRIX entry is needed?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12842:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1179:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Trust Root rehashed for typed_tx / sequencer / transition_ledger. R-022 skip token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12843:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1182:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:76:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate — single state-vs-outcome match arm now; no inner-consistency check.)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12844:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1186:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:88:12. With ResolutionRef now removed (round-5 RQ5), is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed, partially addressed, or unchanged? Specifically: does removing the resolution_ref wrapper make TB-15+ multi-resolver evolution easier (fewer wire fields to reshape) or harder (lost a future-extension hook)?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12845:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1189:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:96:- **R-022 skip-token**: Confirm `OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` is sufficient justification for the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink (the symbol is gone; its role is absorbed by `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12846:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1191:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:191:    f"**Round-5 closures**: RQ3 (chaintape replay smoke) + Q9/RQ6 (type-use fence) + RQ5 (ResolutionRef wrapper drop)\n"
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12847:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1195:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12848:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1205:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12849:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1208:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:273:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12850:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1216:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:314:            ResolutionRef pointing to a system-emitted resolution (no
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12851:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1217:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:317:            ResolutionRef.outcome state != Resolved or if challenge
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12852:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1229:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:423:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12853:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1231:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:425:  - `CompleteSetRedeemTx { tx_id, parent_state_root, event_id, owner, outcome: OutcomeSide, share_amount: ShareAmount, resolution_ref: ResolutionRef, signature }` agent-callable but pre-resolution rejected at sequencer.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12855:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1269:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1172:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12856:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1278:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1213:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12857:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1279:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1214:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12878:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1471:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:3636:   439	    assert!(err.contains("InvalidResolutionRef"));
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12879:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1487:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4062:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12880:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1497:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12881:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1500:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4286:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12882:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1510:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4333:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12883:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1513:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4470:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12884:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1523:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4517:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12885:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1535:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4962:tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:56:    MarketSeedTx, OutcomeSide, ResolutionRef, ShareAmount, TypedTx,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12886:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1622:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12887:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1625:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12888:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1629:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6594:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12889:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1630:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12890:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1632:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6610:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).

The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12896:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1670:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1413:    19	**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12897:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1678:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1454:    60	            ResolutionRef pointing to a system-emitted resolution (no

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12899:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1691:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1563:   169	  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:12900:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1693:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1565:   171	  - `CompleteSetRedeemTx { tx_id, parent_state_root, event_id, owner, outcome: OutcomeSide, share_amount: ShareAmount, resolution_ref: ResolutionRef, signature }` agent-callable but pre-resolution rejected at sequencer.
