handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:47:### Q1 — Rule completeness for B7+ surface
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:51:- (Q1.a) Are there OTHER B7+ surfaces still uncovered by rules? E.g., synthetic_short_circuit field ground-truth-feedback discipline (Q7.b round-2 lesson), AGENT_MODELS env var sanity, BUDGET_MODE env values, etc.?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:52:- (Q1.b) R-014 fires on EVERY *.rs edit (file_glob "*.rs"). Is the constant warn noise acceptable, or should it scope-narrow (e.g., file_glob covering only the 12 .rs files in current Trust Root, plus a per-rule meta about which manifest entries the file may map to)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:53:- (Q1.c) R-018 BLOCK uses file_glob "constitution.md" + check.pattern ".*". It depends on judge.sh special-case BEFORE the *.md skip-list. Is the path-routing logic in judge.sh (lines 50-67) airtight, or could a creative path string (e.g., relative path with `./`, symlink) bypass?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:54:- (Q1.d) R-016 fc_trace_in_commit greps the COMMAND for `FC-trace:`. If user uses `git commit -F /tmp/msg` instead of `-m "..."`, the grep misses the file content. Is this a known limitation worth fixing now or post-A0?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:56:### Q2 — Conformance battery coverage and integrity
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:60:- (Q2.a) Are the 17 witnesses TRULY witnesses (i.e., would fail to compile if the symbol is removed/renamed) or are some only "type_name::<T>()" calls that wouldn't catch behavioral regressions?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:61:- (Q2.b) The 9 ignored stubs each include `panic!()` body — they fire if `--ignored` flag is passed. Is this the right pattern, or should they be `#[ignore]` with `unimplemented!()` or empty body?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:62:- (Q2.c) Cross-crate symbols (Lean4Oracle in minif2f_v4) need a SECOND fc_alignment_conformance.rs in `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/`. A0b ignored this. Is that an acceptable deferral or should A0e cover it?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:63:- (Q2.d) Binary-only symbols (run_swarm/run_oneshot in evaluator.rs) are ignored with rationale. Should A1-A8 atoms refactor evaluator to expose these as lib functions for test access?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:65:### Q3 — Case-law sediment quality
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:69:- (Q3.a) Are the cases TRUE precedents (binding for future similar incidents) or just session-summary docs? CLAUDE.md "Common Law" framing: "宪法高度压缩, 具体裁决查 cases/C-xxx.yaml". Do C-071..C-075 stand as standalone rulings without needing this session's chat history?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:70:- (Q3.b) C-073 (ArchitectAI commit authority) declares scope "ALL files EXCEPT constitution.md" — including PREREG amendments, Trust Root manifest, governance instrumentation. Is this correctly aligned with Art. V.1.2 amendment text, or does it overreach?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:71:- (Q3.c) C-074 (FC-first) overlaps significantly with memory feedback_fc_first_problem_handling.md. Is the duplication acceptable (memory = AI's runtime guide; case = constitutional precedent for future reference)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:72:- (Q3.d) Cross-references between cases (C-071 ↔ C-073, etc.) form a DAG. Any circular reasoning hazard?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:74:### Q4 — Trust Root manifest expansion 20 → 24
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:78:- (Q4.a) Is the inclusion of rules/engine.py sufficient, or should rules/SCHEMA.yaml also be in Trust Root (defines rule format)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:79:- (Q4.b) `.claude/hooks/judge.sh` is included; what about the other hooks (`build-check.sh` PostToolUse + `session-end.sh` Stop hook)? They run on every Edit/Write/Stop respectively. If they're tampered, is the gate weaker?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:80:- (Q4.c) `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` is included. Should ALL tests be in Trust Root (they're the witness battery for behavioral correctness)? Or only the FC-conformance one?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:81:- (Q4.d) Trust Root self-reference: TRACE_MATRIX_v?.md docs themselves are NOT in Trust Root. v2 § 5 records this as future-deferred. Is this acceptable for now or a real gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3366:./handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:32:*   **(Q1.a) Unwired `Ok(response)` sites**: **PASS**. The two `client.generate(...)` call sites in `evaluator.rs` (`run_oneshot:232`, `run_swarm:583`) are both immediately followed by `acc.record_llm_call(...)`. No call paths appear to be un-metered.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3370:./handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_V2_VERDICT_2026-04-24.md:26:Both Phase 9.A and v2 A use identical harness settings (n=8, tx_cap=50, homogeneous algebraic skill); only the DeepSeek model could have changed. Conclusion: **`deepseek-chat` drifted between 2026-04-22 and 2026-04-24**, making `mathd_algebra_246` solvable from seeds that previously failed. This validates v2 Limitation #9 (model drift unverifiable) as a real, observed phenomenon in this experiment.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3470:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3744:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_reaudit.sh:88:- (RQ4.b) The synthetic row's `synthetic_short_circuit` is implicitly absent (None). Any conflict with `synthetic_timeout_or_crash`? Both can't be true simultaneously by construction (timeout means evaluator didn't return at all).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3471:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3778:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_REAUDIT_2026-04-25.md:99:- (RQ4.b) The synthetic row's `synthetic_short_circuit` is implicitly absent (None). Any conflict with `synthetic_timeout_or_crash`? Both can't be true simultaneously by construction (timeout means evaluator didn't return at all).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3491:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:36:-   **(RQ2.a) Synthetic Row Correctness:** The `emit_synthetic_unsolved` function in `run_p0_calibration.sh` correctly generates a JSONL row with `"solved": false`, `"progress": 0`, and `"synthetic_timeout_or_crash": true`. The `solved()` function in `compute_p0.py` correctly interprets this as UNSOLVED by checking `int(row["progress"]) == 1`. The smoke test evidence confirms the treatment run produced a row with `solved: false` and `synthetic_short_circuit: true`, which is handled by the same logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3493:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_reaudit.sh:52:| Q7.a: API drift detection | CHALLENGE | 15b87fb: MODEL_SNAPSHOT (active_model@git_sha[:12]) + BUILD_SHA + canary timestamps |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3494:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_reaudit.sh:88:- (RQ4.b) The synthetic row's `synthetic_short_circuit` is implicitly absent (None). Any conflict with `synthetic_timeout_or_crash`? Both can't be true simultaneously by construction (timeout means evaluator didn't return at all).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3495:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_reaudit.sh:91:- (RQ5.a) `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` env now drift-detection-stamped at runner level. Evaluator was already reading `MODEL_SNAPSHOT` env before B7-extra fix (jsonl_schema.rs:112). The runner's `${ACTIVE_MODEL}@${GIT_SHA:0:12}${GIT_DIRTY}` format works for downstream pairing? `-dirty` suffix when working tree has unstaged changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3502:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:56:- (Q1.b) The `synthetic_short_circuit` field's doc comment warns about cost-asymmetry. Is the warning sufficient, or should the evaluator EMIT a stderr-level WARN at every short-circuit so log-readers also see it (currently `info!` not `warn!`)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3503:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:58:- (Q1.d) Does the `synthetic_short_circuit` flag correctly serialize as `null` (omitted) for control runs and `true` only for treatment? (The smoke confirms this empirically; check the code path.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3504:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3507:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:115:- (Q7.a) If DeepSeek API drifts mid-batch (memory: project_deepseek_drift_2026-04-24 — `deepseek-chat` drifted Apr 24 with mathd_algebra_246 FAIL→SOLVE on same seeds), the resulting p_0 is INCONSISTENT across the run. PREREG § 1.8 commits source_pool_sha256 + heldout_sealed_hash, but NOT model_snapshot. Should we abort + redo if drift detected, or accept noise?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3517:./handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_reaudit.py:56:| Q7.a: API drift mitigation | CHALLENGE | 15b87fb: MODEL_SNAPSHOT (active_model@git_sha[:12]) + BUILD_SHA + canary timestamps logged |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3523:./handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a0_exit_audit.sh:40:- (Q1.a) Are there OTHER B7+ surfaces still uncovered by rules? E.g., synthetic_short_circuit field ground-truth-feedback discipline (Q7.b round-2 lesson), AGENT_MODELS env var sanity, BUDGET_MODE env values, etc.?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3524:./handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a0_exit_audit.sh:51:- (Q2.c) Cross-crate symbols (Lean4Oracle in minif2f_v4) need a SECOND fc_alignment_conformance.rs in `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/`. A0b ignored this. Is that an acceptable deferral or should A0e cover it?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3525:./handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a0_exit_audit.sh:52:- (Q2.d) Binary-only symbols (run_swarm/run_oneshot in evaluator.rs) are ignored with rationale. Should A1-A8 atoms refactor evaluator to expose these as lib functions for test access?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3527:./handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a0_exit_audit.sh:69:- (Q4.c) `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` is included. Should ALL tests be in Trust Root (they're the witness battery for behavioral correctness)? Or only the FC-conformance one?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3537:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3538:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:53:-   **(Q4.c) Counter-argument (measurement artifact)**: **PASS**. This is the correct and necessary framing. The thesis describes the intended production behavior of the system. The calibration is a meta-process to measure a parameter *about* the system. It is acceptable for the measurement apparatus to not perfectly mirror the system under test, as long as the deviation is understood and accounted for. Finding (A) and the `synthetic_short_circuit` flag provide this accounting.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3539:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:60:-   **(Q5.c) Downstream interpretation**: **PASS**. The `synthetic_short_circuit` flag and its associated doc comment (`evaluator.rs:149-151`) are sufficient mitigation for Finding (B) (cost asymmetry). Any downstream tool that misinterprets the cost of these rows is ignoring the explicit warning provided in the data schema.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3561:./handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_PAPER1_VERDICT_2026-04-23.md:45:| P1-11 | Build provenance (commits + model snapshot) | Tag a single release commit for the paper; specify deepseek-chat snapshot date/version. § 1.3 says "snapshot referenced in § 5" but § 5 has no such reference — dangling pointer. | Codex REPRO-1 + Gemini REPRO-1/3 |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4304:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3760:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:58:- (Q1.d) Does the `synthetic_short_circuit` flag correctly serialize as `null` (omitted) for control runs and `true` only for treatment? (The smoke confirms this empirically; check the code path.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4305:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3762:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:78:- (Q3.c) The `solved()` predicate in `compute_p0.py` reads `progress_verified` if present, else `has_golden_path`. For a synthetic_short_circuit row, both will be False (correctly UNSOLVED). For a control row that SOLVED, both will be True. Is the predicate correct under the v2 RunAggregate schema?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4309:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4310:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3786:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:53:-   **(Q4.c) Counter-argument (measurement artifact)**: **PASS**. This is the correct and necessary framing. The thesis describes the intended production behavior of the system. The calibration is a meta-process to measure a parameter *about* the system. It is acceptable for the measurement apparatus to not perfectly mirror the system under test, as long as the deviation is understood and accounted for. Finding (A) and the `synthetic_short_circuit` flag provide this accounting.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4311:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3787:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:60:-   **(Q5.c) Downstream interpretation**: **PASS**. The `synthetic_short_circuit` flag and its associated doc comment (`evaluator.rs:149-151`) are sufficient mitigation for Finding (B) (cost asymmetry). Any downstream tool that misinterprets the cost of these rows is ignoring the explicit warning provided in the data schema.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4404:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5385:Q4: No LLM-as-Judge defect found. Control accepts only through Lean (`verify_omega_detailed` / `verify_partial`) ([evaluator.rs:777](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:777), [line 1016](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1016)). Treatment is a measurement artifact, not a production state transition. That counter-argument is structurally sound, but TRACE should mark the rollback mapping partial, not full ✅ ([TRACE:31](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:31)).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4822:./handover/ai-direct/STEPB_ART_II1_REDESIGN_2026-04-16.md:120:Codex Q2 pointed out tx_count gap could be model differences. True. The A/B design controls for this because both arms use `deepseek-chat`. If tx_count drops dramatically on treatment arm → confirms mechanism was the issue. If tx_count stays high → mechanism wasn't the bottleneck.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8636:Q1: Not complete. Add constitution Bash/empty-edit coverage, expand R-019 to all deprecated aliases and existing defaults, and add rules/tests for the synthetic-short-circuit discipline. R-014 should be scope-narrowed and its message is stale: it still says 20 entries while v2 says 24 ([R-014:13](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/active/R-014_trust_root_manifest_drift.yaml:13), [TRACE v2:61](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:61)).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8638:Q2: The 17 witnesses are valid compile/link witnesses, but many are not behavioral witnesses (`type_name` only at [fc_alignment_conformance.rs:35](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs:35), [fc_alignment_conformance.rs:51](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs:51)). The ignored `panic!()` stubs are acceptable as debt sentinels. The missing minif2f conformance test is not an acceptable deferral. Refactoring `run_swarm/run_oneshot` to lib is appropriate for A1-A8, not necessarily A0.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8640:Q3: C-071..C-075 are mostly real precedents, not just summaries. C-073 aligns with Art. V.1.2’s “any non-constitution file” language ([constitution.md:606](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/constitution.md:606)). C-074 duplication with memory is acceptable. Cross-refs are not a DAG, but the C-071/C-073 cycle is not fatal because both independently anchor to constitution lines.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8642:Q4: `rules/engine.py` and `judge.sh` inclusion is necessary and good. `rules/SCHEMA.yaml` can remain deferred while unused by `engine.py` ([engine.py:18](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/rules/engine.py:18)). `build-check.sh` and `session-end.sh` are lower priority but should enter the next Trust Root expansion because they are active hooks ([settings.json:15](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/settings.json:15), [settings.json:27](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/settings.json:27)). Do not put all tests in Trust Root, but do add the Trust Root immutability test if it is the rollback guard.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:51:**Document split**: this packet is the **stable current-state snapshot** of Phase A at exit — it describes WHAT IS, not how it got here. The chronological history of audit rounds + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,_R7,...}.md`. Reviewers needing closure-of-prior-finding context read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:177:### Q1 — Code correctness spot-checks
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:178:- (Q1.a) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` mint a single `run_id` at function entry and pass it to BOTH `fc_trace::emit_event` and `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier? Verify by reading `run_swarm` and `run_oneshot` entry points + `make_pput` signature.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:179:- (Q1.b) Does `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py::detect_provider` route `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` to `siliconflow` (not `deepseek`)? Verify by reading the function + checking that `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` includes `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:180:- (Q1.c) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail closed when `python3` is missing? Verify by reading the test source (appended to this packet) + checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:182:### Q2 — PREREG amendment soundness
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:183:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:184:- (Q2.b) Re-calibration conditions in § 3 are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run, not guarantees that calibration completes before any specific phase. Is this framed correctly without claiming calibration MUST run before Phase E?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:185:- (Q2.c) The amendment's SHA-256 is in Trust Root. Does `boot::tests::verify_trust_root_passes_on_intact_repo` pass cleanly?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:187:### Q3 — Atomicity, FC-trace discipline, governance

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:189:- (Q3.b) 5 cases C-071..C-075 sediment 2026-04-25 session decisions as constitutional precedent. Are the rulings standalone-readable + correctly cross-referenced?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5431:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5433:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5434:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5435:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5436:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5437:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5486:| `run_id::mint_run_id` correlation key + 9 wired FC events (A6 + A8e F4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | A6 introduced a per-run correlation id (originally named `run_corr_id`, now unified into `run_id`) anchoring all events. A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) split this out into a dedicated module + threaded the same id into `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier (no ms drift). Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). End-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (forced because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read). | ✅ |

Reviewers needing closure-of-prior-finding context read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7441:162:- (Q5.c) Does this packet contain ANY round-N retrospective text, fix-shipped headers, "previous round caught X" claims, or historical lineage like "(post-A8e F4)" / "(added by A8eN)" / "(chain position N via A8e)" — anywhere? It should NOT — those belong only in the history doc.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7447:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:21:2.  **Schema Drift from B1 Contract (Q5.c)**: The B1 `RunAggregate` v2 schema mandates a `progress: u8` field. The B4 implementation in `PputResult` emits `verified: Option<bool>` (`evaluator.rs:90`). While semantically similar, this is a direct violation of the frozen schema's type and name contract. Downstream tooling built against the B1 spec will break or require shims.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7448:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:*   **(Q4.a) Round-trip preserved**: **PASS**. All new fields in `PputResult` are `Option<T>` and use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` (`evaluator.rs:82-93`). This is the canonical and correct way to ensure that deserializing old data and re-serializing it produces identical output.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7449:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:74:*   **(Q5.c) `progress: u8` vs `verified: Option<bool>`**: **CHALLENGE (Major Bug)**. This is a clear deviation. The B1 `RunAggregate` schema specifies `progress: u8`. The B4 implementation adds `verified: Option<bool>` (`evaluator.rs:90`). The emitted JSONL will have a field named `verified` with a boolean value, not a field named `progress` with a numeric value. This breaks the B1 contract and will fail validation against a strict schema-checker.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7462:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.sh:83:- (Q5.c) RunAggregate has `progress: u8` (Lean ground truth). PputResult does NOT have `progress` — only `verified: Option<bool>`. Is this gap intentional (B5 will fill it) or a bug?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7519:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:1478:// Codex#2 + Gemini Q4 (A8 round-1 audit): `run_corr_id` was generated
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7633:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5107:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7634:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5108:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7635:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5109:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7636:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7718:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6762:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:55:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7719:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6766:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:80:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. Acceptable uses: round-1/2/3 retrospective text describing what the packet USED to say.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7720:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6767:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:83:- (RQ13) Verify packet self-consistency: any other "conservative" claims about the substitution? Any other anchor-count mismatches? Any other contradictions between round-1 questions and round-2/3 closures?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6852:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7727:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6853:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7728:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6854:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7770:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8144:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7771:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8145:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7772:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8146:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7788:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:93:- (Q5.c) RunAggregate has `progress: u8` (Lean ground truth). PputResult does NOT have `progress` — only `verified: Option<bool>`. Is this gap intentional (B5 will fill it) or a bug?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7965:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4640:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.sh:83:- (Q5.c) RunAggregate has `progress: u8` (Lean ground truth). PputResult does NOT have `progress` — only `verified: Option<bool>`. Is this gap intentional (B5 will fill it) or a bug?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7966:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4642:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:21:2.  **Schema Drift from B1 Contract (Q5.c)**: The B1 `RunAggregate` v2 schema mandates a `progress: u8` field. The B4 implementation in `PputResult` emits `verified: Option<bool>` (`evaluator.rs:90`). While semantically similar, this is a direct violation of the frozen schema's type and name contract. Downstream tooling built against the B1 spec will break or require shims.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7967:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4645:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:*   **(Q4.a) Round-trip preserved**: **PASS**. All new fields in `PputResult` are `Option<T>` and use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` (`evaluator.rs:82-93`). This is the canonical and correct way to ensure that deserializing old data and re-serializing it produces identical output.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7968:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4649:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:74:*   **(Q5.c) `progress: u8` vs `verified: Option<bool>`**: **CHALLENGE (Major Bug)**. This is a clear deviation. The B1 `RunAggregate` schema specifies `progress: u8`. The B4 implementation adds `verified: Option<bool>` (`evaluator.rs:90`). The emitted JSONL will have a field named `verified` with a boolean value, not a field named `progress` with a numeric value. This breaks the B1 contract and will fail validation against a strict schema-checker.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7970:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4677:handover/audits/run_gemini_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.py:87:- (Q5.c) RunAggregate has `progress: u8` (Lean ground truth). PputResult does NOT have `progress` — only `verified: Option<bool>`. Is this gap intentional (B5 will fill it) or a bug?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7974:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4777:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:93:- (Q5.c) RunAggregate has `progress: u8` (Lean ground truth). PputResult does NOT have `progress` — only `verified: Option<bool>`. Is this gap intentional (B5 will fill it) or a bug?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8028:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:6329:Q4/Q5: Backward read compatibility is okay for old rows through `LegacyRunAggregate` flattening, but new rows are also legacy-classified because evaluator emits no `schema_version`. Field spellings present in `PputResult` match v2 names, but the set misses `progress`, `rollback_count`, guardrails, model snapshot, mode, etc. `verified: Option<bool>` is not a substitute for `progress: u8`.

Reviewers needing closure-of-prior-finding context read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8216:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5404:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8217:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5406:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8221:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5410:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8222:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5411:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8231:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5716:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8232:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5718:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8233:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5719:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8561:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:1596:// Codex#2 + Gemini Q4 (A8 round-1 audit): `run_corr_id` was generated
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8677:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5273:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8678:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5274:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8679:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5275:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8680:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5276:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8681:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5277:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8682:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5278:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
