handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8949:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:86:## Round-3 fixes shipped (`A8e2`, commit `<pending>`)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8950:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

A8e2 adds `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` to TR, raising the count to 34.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8959:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8960:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:189:- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8961:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:212:- New module `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8962:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:213:- 9 wired anchor sites total (round-1 had 6 — A8e fix F4 added 3 swarm verify sites): synthetic short-circuit / mr tick / OMEGA full-proof / OMEGA per-tactic / natural MaxTxExhausted (with budget_regime payload from A5) / oneshot FC1-N12 verify bracket / **swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (alone + tape+payload retry)** / **swarm `verify_partial`**.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8963:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:220:- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env names per provider; `get_client_round_robin` distributes via `_rr_counters` mod `len(clients)`. `/stats` exposes `per_key_requests` for observability. New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8964:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:245:(Round-1 risk #5 "No FC1-N12 emit in run_swarm verify path" was CLOSED by A8e fix F4 — both auditors flagged the staleness in round-2 (Codex R2#3, Gemini R2#2). Removed from this list.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8965:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:250:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8966:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8967:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8968:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:392:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8969:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9108:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5071:// FC_TRACE_FILE=<path> code path in a fresh OnceLock state. Production
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9109:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5102:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9110:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5105:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9111:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5106:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9112:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5107:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9113:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5108:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9114:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9115:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5150:| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9116:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5158:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9124:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5416:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9125:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5421:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:26:- Cumulative tests: **264 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed** (was 261; +3 run_id unit tests).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9126:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5423:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9127:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5424:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:48:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9142:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5623:    12	- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9164:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.

Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9181:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:6381:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9198:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7656:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:8:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9199:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7658:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9200:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7659:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:12:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9201:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7660:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9202:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7661:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9212:handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_V2_1_VERDICT_2026-04-25.md:31:| v2.1-P0-E | Stabilize artifacts — final commit/tag, move jsonl out of `.claude/worktrees/` | 12 jsonl copied to `handover/evidence/v2/` | partially closed (P1, tag still pending) | closed |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9217:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9400:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5056:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9917:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9342:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10003:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10313:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10005:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10315:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10006:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10316:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10558:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5807:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:72:**TL;DR**: when a Q7.b "synthetic UNSOLVED on any non-zero exit" was added in round-1 fix to address sampling bias, it silently absorbed TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panics into "valid" calibration data — neutralizing the B1 fix that the same round was supposed to deliver. **Codex caught it in round-2; Gemini missed it (PASS).** Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`, conservative reading wins → VETO. Round-2 fix (commit `1df1f62`) discriminates exit codes: only timeout (124) emits synthetic row; any other crash ABORT BATCH with grep for TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED. Round-3 Gemini returned CHALLENGE on a follow-up exhaustiveness gap (EXIT=0 + empty PPUT_RESULT case fell through to generic crash branch); fixed in same notepad-update cycle. **Lesson**: when fixing a sampling-bias bug, the fix itself can become a security bypass; always re-audit fixes before promoting to PASS. The dual-audit's value is exactly in this kind of cross-checking.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10559:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5812:./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3:**Session Summary**: B7 (Trust Root + Boot freeze) → 用户 atomic-alignment critique (3 flowcharts) → B7 alignment fix → B7-extra rollback toggle + calibration scripts → dual audit round-1 VETO/VETO → 13-fix landing → simplifier pass → **constitution amendment (sudo)**: V.1.1 sudo scope + V.1.2 ArchitectAI commit authority + V.1.3 JudgeAI→Veto-AI + V.3 amendment log → re-audit round 2 VETO/PASS (Codex caught self-inflicted regression: Q7.b silently absorbed TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panics) → round-2 fix → re-audit round 3 CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE (problem_file_missing absorption + boot preflight `||true` exit-discard + EXIT=0+empty PPUT_RESULT non-exhaustive) → round-3 fix (commit `d0d474e`) → **round-4 audit in flight**. **187/187 cargo test PASS** + 20 ignored. Trust Root manifest **20 files** (was 15 — added main.rs / Cargo.lock / runner.sh / compute_p0.py per audit). User authorized auto-research overnight to PROCEED on PASS/PASS.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10927:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5431:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10928:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5433:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10929:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5434:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10931:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5436:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10932:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5437:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10933:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11029:handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a0_exit_audit.py:47:- (Q2.a) Are witnesses TRULY witnesses (would fail compile if symbol removed) or just `type_name::<T>()` calls?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11097:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11143:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5389:Q6: Abort is currently manual, not enforced. Borderline `0.05 < p₀ ≤ 0.10` should not trigger post-hoc retuning to tx75; that would violate the frozen protocol. If you want a yellow-band rule, define it before launch.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11280:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3537:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.

It should because `CompleteSetMintTx` appears on the use-line itself, even though the bound name is `Foo`.

    97	## Specifically scrutinize
    98	
    99	**Round-5 RQ3 smoke determinism**: The smoke pre-computes the post-mint state_root via `complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx)`. The test asserts both txs land (`l4_entries >= 2`) and the live alice balance post = 102M. Confirm the pre-computation contract is stable.

   101	**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.

    14	An audit of the TuringOS v4 TB-13 implementation follows.
    15	
    16	---
    17	
    18	### **Executive Summary**
    19	
    20	This round-5 audit re-evaluates TB-13 after the `d3473bb` commit, which addresses two substantive challenges raised by Codex in the previous round. My review finds that both fixes are not only correct but represent significant structural improvements that harden the implementation and its evidentiary basis.
    21	
    22	1.  The new `tb_13_scan_lines()` helper for the forward-fence (Q9/RQ6) correctly closes a loophole where an unmarked file using TB-13 types could have bypassed Layer 2 scans. The logic is sound, providing a robust fallback that does not rely on developer discipline with authoring markers.
    23	2.  The new `manual_replay_from_disk()` test helper (RQ3) provides direct, byte-for-byte proof of map equality for the new economic state fields. This replaces a weaker, inferential proof based on state-root matching and provides airtight evidence of replay determinism for non-empty TB-13 state.
    24	
    25	All architect-mandated implementation questions (Q1-Q9) continue to pass, with some gates now backed by stronger evidence. My primary architectural concern from round-3 (Q12, redeem-mechanism coupling) was fully resolved by the round-5 `ResolutionRef` removal, which has resulted in a cleaner and more extensible design. The project is in a robust state for shipment.
    26	
    27	---
    28	
    29	### **Round-6-Specific Risks to Scrutinize**
    30	
    31	#### **Q9/RQ6 (R4) Layer 2 fix**
    32	
    33	**Finding**: PASS. The new `tb_13_scan_lines()` helper at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:165..183` is robust.
    34	
    35	-   **Partially-marked file classification**: The conditional `source.lines().any(is_tb_13_authoring_marker)` is the correct test under the project's trace-matrix discipline. If a file contains *any* TB-13 authoring marker, it is classified as a "marker-file," and only its explicitly marked TB-13 spans are scanned by the Layer 2 forbidden-token list. Unmarked code within that same file is, by definition, not TB-13 code and is correctly excluded from the Layer 2 scan. The most critical security boundary—the Layer 1 `HARD_BANNED_LEGACY_IMPORTS` check—still runs on the entire file, preventing any legacy `use` statements regardless of marker placement. This logic is sound.
    36	-   **Removal of `src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs` from `FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR`**: This is safe and correct. The file was removed because the new Layer 2 logic (scanning all non-comment lines of unmarked files) would have caused a false positive on string literals inside the dashboard's own test fixtures. The file currently has no TB-13 markers or type uses. If it is extended in the future to include TB-13 contributions, it will be automatically re-added to the fence's scope by either `discover_by_marker()` (if a marker is added) or `discover_by_type_use()` (if it uses a TB-13 type). The auto-discovery mechanism correctly justifies its removal from the static floor list.
    37	
    38	#### **RQ3 (R4) direct map-equality fix**
    39	
    40	**Finding**: PASS. The evidence provided by `manual_replay_from_disk()` in `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs` is airtight.
    41	
    42	-   **(a) Full sub-field reconstruction**: The test asserts full equality on `economic_state_t` (`replayed_q.economic_state_t == live_q.economic_state_t`). As `EconomicState` at `src/state/q_state.rs:158` is a struct containing all 13 economic sub-fields, a direct `==` comparison constitutes a byte-for-byte equality check on all of them, not just the TB-13 ones. This is a complete and direct proof.
    43	-   **(b) Non-determinism**: The state path correctly uses `BTreeMap` for all map types (`src/state/q_state.rs`), which has a deterministic iteration order sorted by key. Other types in the state path (`MicroCoin`, `Hash`, etc.) are also deterministic. There are no sources of non-determinism (like `HashMap` iteration) that would invalidate the byte-equal comparison.
    44	-   **(c) Faithful mirror of `verify_chaintape`**: The manual replay helper faithfully reconstructs the exact environment that `verify_chaintape` uses: it opens the `Git2LedgerWriter`, loads `initial_q_state.json`, decodes `pinned_pubkeys.json`, opens the `CasStore`, and calls the public `replay_full_transition` API at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:430`. This is not a mock; it is a direct invocation of the same replay engine against the same on-disk artifacts. The evidence is therefore direct and conclusive.
    45	
    46	#### **R-022 skip-token continuity**
    47	
    48	**Finding**: PASS. The round-6 commit `d3473bb` introduces no new public symbol removals. The only relevant removal was `ResolutionRef` in the prior round-5 commit, which is correctly justified by the skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
    49	
    50	---
    51	
    52	### **Architect Part A §4 + charter §3 Atom 6 mandated audit questions (Q1-Q9)**
    53	
    54	1.  **Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money?**
    55	    **Finding**: PASS. No. The logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1808` remains a pure balance↔collateral migration. The 6-holding sum in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:158` correctly includes `conditional_collateral_t`, ensuring `assert_total_ctf_conserved` passes.
    56	
    57	2.  **Can Redeem fire without a system-emitted resolution?**
    58	    **Finding**: PASS. No. The gate at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` checks `task_markets_t` for a resolved state (`Finalized` or `Bankrupt`), correctly rejecting with `RedeemBeforeResolution` otherwise.
    59	
    60	3.  **Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check?**
    61	    **Finding**: PASS. No. The round-5 removal of `ResolutionRef` simplified and hardened this gate. The `match` arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889` now directly compares `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` tuple correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path.
    62	
    63	4.  **Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx?**
    64	    **Finding**: PASS. Yes. All three new transaction types represent balanced transfers between coin holdings (balances ↔ collateral). The 6-holding sum in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:158` is correct.
    65	
    66	5.  **Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` (MIN-semantics) hold after every transition?**
    67	    **Finding**: PASS. Yes. The MIN-semantics implementation at `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:289` is the correct form. Per the round-3 remediation, this assertion is now called live from all three TB-13 dispatch arms in `src/state/sequencer.rs`, ensuring it is not just a test-time check.
    68	
    69	6.  **Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance?**
    70	    **Finding**: PASS. No. The check at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1959` correctly rejects with `InsufficientBalanceForMint`.
    71	
    72	7.  **Are conditional shares anywhere counted as Coin?**
    73	    **Finding**: PASS. No. `total_supply_micro` at `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:158` correctly excludes `conditional_share_balances_t` from the sum, per architect CR-13.3.
    74	
    75	8.  **Could a malformed `ShareAmount` underflow at redeem?**
    76	    **Finding**: PASS. No. `ShareAmount` at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1105` is a `u128` newtype. The `RedeemMoreThanOwned` check at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1907` prevents subtraction that would underflow.
    77	
    78	9.  **Forward-fence: does any new TB-13 module file import legacy `prediction_market`?**
    79	    **Finding**: PASS. No. The fence at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` is now stronger. The addition of `discover_by_type_use` provides a robust secondary check that does not rely on developer discipline with authoring markers, significantly reducing the bypass surface.
    80	
    81	---
    82	
    83	### **Architectural Strategic Questions (Class 3 review)**
    84	
    85	10. **Does CompleteSet schema extend cleanly to TB-14 PriceIndex?**
    86	    **Finding**: PASS. Yes. The `conditional_share_balances_t` schema at `src/state/q_state.rs:511` allows for aggregation of total YES/NO depth per `EventId`. While iterating over all agents might be inefficient for markets with a very large number of participants, it is a correct and sufficient data structure for deriving a price index as a read-only view.
    87	
    88	11. **Does the `EventId == TaskId` 1:1 simplification hold up under TB-14+?**
    89	    **Finding**: PASS. Yes. The implementation of `EventId` as a newtype wrapper around `TaskId` (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1088`) is the correct architectural pattern. It provides type safety within TB-13 while making it straightforward to change the inner representation of `EventId` in the future without breaking function signatures.
    90	
    91	12. **With ResolutionRef now removed (round-5 RQ5), is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed?**
    92	    **Finding**: PASS (Fully Addressed). Yes. My previous challenge noted that coupling redemption to specific transaction types via `ResolutionRef` was brittle. By removing `ResolutionRef` and having the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` check the canonical `task_markets_t.state` enum directly, the design is now much more extensible and robust. If a future TB introduces a new resolution mechanism, it only needs to mutate `task_markets_t.state` to a resolved value; the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` logic will not need to be changed. This is a significant architectural improvement that makes future evolution easier.
    93	
    94	13. **Is the MIN-semantics `assert_complete_set_balanced` invariant the right form?**
    95	    **Finding**: PASS. Yes. The MIN-semantics form is correct, as it properly accounts for the post-resolution state where losing-side shares are stranded. Adversarial sequences like "redeem-and-remint" are blocked by the `EventNotOpen` gate added in a previous round (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1823`), which prevents minting into a resolved event. The invariant appears robust.
    96	
    97	---
    98	
    99	### **Final Verdict**
   100	
   101	-   **VERDICT**: **PASS**
   102	-   **Conviction**: **High**
   103	-   **Recommendation**: **PROCEED to SHIP**
   104	
   105	**Rationale**: The TB-13 implementation is in an excellent state. The round-6 fixes have not only closed the specific challenges raised by Codex but have resulted in a simpler, more robust, and more extensible design. The addition of direct, cryptographic proof for non-empty replay determinism provides strong assurance. My primary architectural concern from the previous round has been fully resolved by the removal of `ResolutionRef`. There are no remaining VETO or CHALLENGE-level issues.

Is that correct, or should partially-marked files be treated as fully-in-scope? My read: marker-only-spans is the correct behavior (we don't want to false-positive on TB-12 baseline code that happens to share a file with TB-13 additions). Confirm.

   175	    TB-13 Atom 6 round-6 — Codex R4 remediation (Q9/RQ6 Layer 2 + RQ3 direct map equality)
   176	    
   177	    Codex R4 surfaced two NEW CHALLENGES post-round-5 (no VETO; Gemini R4
   178	    PASS but conservative-verdict-wins per feedback_dual_audit_conflict).
   179	    Per feedback_audit_obs_bias both qualified for surgical fix, not OBS:
   180	    
   181	    **Q9/RQ6 (R4)** — Layer 2 scans use marker-scoped tb_13_spans()
   182	    Codex found that round-5's type-use discovery added unmarked TB-13
   183	    files to effective_fence_scope, but Layer 2 (FORBIDDEN_LEGACY_TOKENS +
   184	    f64) still walked tb_13_spans() — which returns nothing for unmarked
   185	    files. An unmarked file importing CompleteSetMintTx and using f64 would
   186	    be discovered into scope but never scanned.
   187	    
   188	    Fix: new tb_13_scan_lines() helper. For marker-files → return spans
   189	    (preserves doc-xref skip); for unmarked-discovered files → return all
   190	    non-comment lines. Wired into both legacy_cpm_api_not_imported_by_complete_set
   191	    Layer 2 and no_f64_in_complete_set_or_market_seed.
   192	    
   193	    Side effect: src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs was in FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR but
   194	    has 0 TB-13 markers + 0 TB-13 type uses (Atom 4 §13 dashboard
   195	    DEFERRED to TB-14). With the new walker, its negative-list test fixture
   196	    (price_yes / price_no string literals at line 1628-29) would false-
   197	    positive. Removed from FLOOR — will be auto-rediscovered by
   198	    discover_by_marker when TB-14 ships dashboard contributions.
   199	    
   200	    New unit test tb_13_scan_lines_handles_marker_and_unmarked_files asserts
   201	    both branches (marker-file uses spans + skips xref; unmarked-file uses
   202	    non-comment lines + filters pure comments).
   203	    
   204	    **RQ3 (R4)** — overclaim of state-root equality as 'cryptographic proof
   205	    of map equality'
   206	    
   207	    Codex was right: state-root mutators hash domain || prev_root ||
   208	    canonical_tx, NOT the full QState. State-root equality proves
   209	    deterministic tx-chain replay; map equality follows by dispatch-purity
   210	    inference, not directly.
   211	    
   212	    Fix: stronger evidence via direct map-equality assertion. Added
   213	    manual_replay_from_disk() helper that opens Git2LedgerWriter +
   214	    loads initial_q_state.json + decodes pinned_pubkeys.json + opens
   215	    CasStore + calls replay_full_transition (pub API). The smoke now
   216	    asserts:
   217	    - replayed_q.economic_state_t.conditional_collateral_t == live (byte-equal)
   218	    - replayed_q.economic_state_t.conditional_share_balances_t == live (byte-equal)
   219	    - full economic_state_t == live (belt-and-suspenders)
   220	    in addition to the existing state_root_hex match.
   221	    
   222	    The smoke + README + module docstring wording revised to state the
   223	    correct claim: state-root match proves chain determinism; direct map
   224	    equality proves byte-equal reconstruction. Round-5 closure language
   225	    ('cryptographic proof of full QState equality via state_root') was
   226	    overclaimed; round-6 says what the evidence actually proves.
   227	    
   228	    Trust Root: no Rust src/ change; only tests/ + handover/. No manifest
   229	    rehash.
   230

   232	      (round-5 baseline 791 + 1 tb_13_scan_lines_handles_marker_and_unmarked_files).
   233	    cargo test --test tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence = 5/5.
   234	    cargo test --test tb_13_chaintape_smoke = 1/1 (with direct map equality).
   235

   237	    direct map equality) + FC3-Alignment (forward-fence Layer 2 coverage
   238	    extended to non-marker-discovered scope).

    75	**Q1-Q8**: Round-6 doesn't touch dispatch arms, monetary invariants, share semantics, or seed solvency. PASS expected.
    76	
    77	**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Round-6 strengthens the Layer 2 coverage. Re-evaluate.
    78	
    79	## Specifically scrutinize
    80	
    81	**Trust Root**: round-6 modified only `tests/` and `handover/`. No Rust src/ change. Trust Root manifest unchanged. Confirm no rehash needed.
    82	
    83	**No new pub symbols**: round-6 added `tb_13_scan_lines`, `manual_replay_from_disk` — these are private (test-only). Confirm no R-022 surface.
    84	
    85	**Test count drift**: round-5 baseline 791 → round-6 baseline 792 (+1 = `tb_13_scan_lines_handles_marker_and_unmarked_files`). Confirm.
    86	
    87	## Verdict format
    88	
    89	```text
    90	## VERDICT: PASS
    91	- conviction: low/medium/high
    92	- recommendation: PROCEED to SHIP
    93	```
    94	
    95	```text
    96	## VERDICT: CHALLENGE
    97	- <claim id> CHALLENGE: <one-line reason + line refs>
    98	- conviction: low/medium/high
    99	- recommendation: FIX-THEN-PROCEED  (if cheap fix exists) or PROCEED-WITH-OBS (if architecturally deferred)
   100	```
   101	
   102	```text
   103	## VERDICT: VETO
   104	- <claim id> VETO: <one-line BLOCKING reason + line refs>
   105	- conviction: low/medium/high
   106	(VETO blocks ship per feedback_dual_audit_conflict; VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS.)
   107	```
   108	
   109	Cite file:line for every finding.
   110	
   111	Save your audit to: handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md.

   119	EXIT=$?
   120	
   121	if [ $EXIT -ne 0 ]; then

   123	  echo "  partial output saved to $OUT.raw" >&2
   124	fi
   125	
   126	mv "$OUT.raw" "$OUT"
   127	echo "  Audit saved: $OUT" >&2
   128	exit $EXIT

  1916	
  1917	            // Step 5: state_root advance.
  1918	            q_next.state_root_t = market_seed_accept_state_root(&q.state_root_t, tx);
  1919	
  1920	            Ok((q_next, SignalBundle::default()))
  1921	        }
  1922	    }
  1923	}
  1924	
  1925	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1926	// CO1.7-extra D2: advance_head_t — post-commit head_t close (Art 0.4)
  1927	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1928	
  1929	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 5 — L4 sequencer post-commit head_t wiring (Art 0.4).
  1930	///
  1931	/// Closes the G-1 carry-forward: when `writer` surfaces a commit OID hex
  1932	/// (Git2LedgerWriter), advance `q.head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(hex)`;
  1933	/// when `writer` returns None (InMemoryLedgerWriter), leave `q.head_t`
  1934	/// unchanged (no-op preservation).
  1935	///
  1936	/// Called from `apply_one` stage 9 AFTER `writer.commit` succeeds. Pure
  1937	/// function (writer is `&dyn` so behavior depends only on writer's
  1938	/// `head_commit_oid_hex` return + q's prior state).
  1939	///
  1940	/// **Visibility** (CO1.7-extra round-3 B2): `pub` (NOT `pub(crate)`) so that
  1941	/// flat integration tests under `tests/co1_7_extra_*.rs` per round-2 MF5 can
  1942	/// call this helper directly.
  1943	///
  1944	/// **Atomicity** (CO1.7-extra round-2 MF9): in apply_one, called under the
  1945	/// `q_w` write lock immediately after `writer.commit` returns Ok. For Git2
  1946	/// (Some path), this is post-commit non-failing best-effort head binding —
  1947	/// `q.head_t`, `q.ledger_root_t`, and `next_logical_t` advance atomically.
  1948	/// For InMemory (None path), this is explicit no-op preservation —
  1949	/// `q.head_t` stays at the value `*q_w = q_next` left it (which equals the
  1950	/// prior value because pure transition bodies never mutate head_t per
  1951	/// CO1.7 K3 v1.2).
  1952	pub fn advance_head_t(q: &mut QState, writer: &dyn LedgerWriter) {
  1953	    if let Some(commit_oid_hex) = writer.head_commit_oid_hex() {
  1954	        q.head_t = crate::state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex);
  1955	    }
  1956	}
  1957	
  1958	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1959	// Submission types — K1 dual counter
  1960	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1961	
  1962	/// Returned by `Sequencer::submit`. Carries `submit_id` (always assigned at
  1963	/// submit time) but **NOT** `logical_t` — logical_t is only assigned post-accept
  1964	/// per K1 (see spec § 3 + CO1.7 K1 closure).
  1965	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
  1966	pub struct SubmissionReceipt {
  1967	    pub submit_id: u64,
  1968	}
  1969	
  1970	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-S3: L4 sequencer queue payload carrying both `submit_id`
  1971	/// and the typed tx through to `apply_one`.
  1972	///
  1973	/// Required by P1:6 / TB-2 charter §1: the L4.E rejection-evidence ledger
  1974	/// keys rejected submissions by `submit_id` (NOT `logical_t`), so the
  1975	/// sequencer driver loop must observe the same `submit_id` the caller received.
